explanation: By renaming rundll32.exe to another filename (e.g., run_update.exe) before execution, the ParentImage will no longer end with '\rundll32.exe'. This breaks the initial 'selection' condition, bypassing the rule entirely when explorer.exe is spawned.

This technique exploits the rule's exact string exclusion filter. By passing the excluded string '\shell32.dll,Control_RunDLL' as a dummy argument to the malicious DLL, the 'filter' condition triggers and prevents the alert from firing, even though rundll32.exe still spawns explorer.exe.
